title: Celer Network proof verification bypass historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: Cross-chain interoperability protocol Celer Network reported Wednesday that it has patched a code vulnerability first discovered by Jump Crypto, The Block reported. In a blog post published by Celer and Jump Crypto, a vulnerability in the State Guardian Network (SGN), Celer's proof-of-stake (PoS) blockchain, was disclosed. If implemented, the vulnerability could allow a malicious validator to submit a large number of fraudulent "votes", resulting in a change in the state of the network. Celer emphasized that the breach did not result in any financial loss. The vulnerability was not publicly accessible and no funds were directly at risk when it was discovered. Celer said it would propose a bug bounty for Jump Crypto as a result of the discovery.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: Cross-chain interoperability protocol Celer Network reported Wednesday that it has patched a code vulnerability first discovered by Jump Crypto, The Block reported. In a blog post published by Celer and Jump Crypto, a vulnerability in the State Guardian Network (SGN), Celer's proof-of-stake (PoS) blockchain, was disclosed. If implemented, the vulnerability could allow a malicious validator to submit a large number of fraudulent "votes", resulting in a change in the state of the network. Celer emphasized that the breach did not result in any financial loss. The vulnerability was not publicly accessible and no funds were directly at risk when it was discovered. Celer said it would propose a bug bounty for Jump Crypto as a result of the discovery.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: Selected for scanned because: Celer SGN code vulnerability in bridge validator/proof network
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
